description:
## What
Serve a persona's public posts as RSS 2.0 and Atom 1.0 feeds. No auth required. Only `public` and `unlisted` visibility posts. Limit to 20 most recent. Include title, description, pubDate, guid (post URI), content:encoded (rendered HTML). No new deps — pure string generation with `format!`. Content-Type: `application/rss+xml; charset=utf-8` and `application/atom+xml; charset=utf-8`.

## Why
Feed readers and aggregators can subscribe to individual persona output without needing an ActivityPub client, improving reach and interoperability.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: review RSS 2.0 spec (https://www.rssboard.org/rss-specification) and Atom 1.0 spec (RFC 4287) for required and optional elements; check what mastodon.social emits for its RSS feed at /users/:username.rss as a live reference shape
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add axum routes for `/users/:username/feed.rss` and `/users/:username/feed.atom`, query DB for up to 20 most recent public/unlisted posts, escape all user-provided content (display name, bio, post content) for XML safety, build feed string with `format!`, return with correct Content-Type header
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration tests that fetch the feed endpoints, parse the returned XML, and validate structure against RSS 2.0 and Atom 1.0 required elements; test that DMs and followers-only posts are excluded; test XML escaping with posts containing `<`, `>`, `&`, `"`

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: RSS 2.0 spec (https://www.rssboard.org/rss-specification) and Atom 1.0 spec (RFC 4287) define required elements authoritatively; mastodon.social live feed at /users/:username.rss provides a real-world reference shape
- Test approach: parse returned XML with a Rust XML parser (quick-xml), assert required elements present and non-empty; assert non-public posts absent; assert XML-escaped content in title/description elements

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Escape `<`, `>`, `&`, `"` in all user-provided fields (display name, bio, post content) — XSS in RSS readers is a real attack surface
- Do NOT include DMs or followers-only posts
- Return 404 if persona username does not exist
title: Implement GET /users/:username/feed.rss and /users/:username/feed.atom endpoints
